additional_traces.rationale: PASS criterion: final-state latency list length is 1 for iteration-2-only delta; FAIL criterion: length 2 implies iteration-1 accumulator carry-over.
PASS criterion: iteration 1 snapshot has has_errors=true and iteration 2 has has_errors=false; FAIL criterion: missing or inconsistent snapshots indicate observability regression.
PASS criterion: both tool responses set llm_calls_made=true, proving async dispatch path each iteration; FAIL criterion: false indicates routing mismatch and invalid FM-14 inference.

recommendations.action: Retain the generic exception FM-14 safeguard in REPLTool (flush_fn + LAST_REPL_RESULT write) and treat removal as a regression gate.

resolution.notes: PASS: The generic except Exception branch now flushes accumulators and writes LAST_REPL_RESULT, so the historical FM-14 source bug is addressed in current code.

recommendations.action: Keep a dedicated escaped-exception test separate from this fixture to verify FM-14 behavior when errors occur after dispatch but before normal return.

resolution.notes: PASS: TestReplExceptionFlushFn monkeypatches LocalREPL.execute_code_async to raise RuntimeError after dispatch, exercising REPLTool's generic exception handling and validating flush/observability writes.

recommendations.action: Extend TestReplExceptionThenRetry with assertions for LAST_REPL_RESULT fields and OBS_WORKER_DISPATCH_LATENCY_MS final delta to make no-drift evidence explicit.

resolution.notes: FAIL: Current coverage infers flush behavior indirectly; direct assertions on observability keys are still missing for this fixture.

recommendations.action: Update Team L showboat notes to include this fixture explicitly and clarify it is a no-drift recovery scenario, not a direct escaped-exception injector.
